task.domains: (topic task) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c task oceTask-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)
task.title: 04-T-002 Teared flex detected (inform the key operator/customer)

abstract.p: You received this teared flex trigger on your worklist as information to prepare your visit, shorten your diagnostic time and to inform the key operator to prevent future broken printheads.

section.title: Preparation
section.p: During job preparation, this information should be used to arrange service parts up-front.
E.g. Prevent a second visit on a missing printhead.

note.p: Be aware and inform the customer that PH replacements caused by a teared flex are not covered by warranty or service contract (depending on the service contract, for example, Procare+ covers the replacement of 1 printhead/year).

section.title: Background information
section.p: The main cause of a teared flex is non-prescribed manual maintenance. In many cases it’s caused by misusing the swab-stick during cleaning of the matrix-plate (see also Information bulletin 1061 of C1630).
Misusing the swab-stick will result in a teared flex as shown in the example below.
The trigger is based on off-line processed ORS printhead information.
Each time the offline algorithm detects a teared flex for a specific printhead, a trigger is generated and will be added to your worklist.
The first trigger appearance in your ADAM worklist does not per definition indicate that the teared flex occurrence happened in the last 2-week period.
The trigger will automatically be removed when teared flexes are not detected anymore.
